weekNoteHtml:
How to use this page. Three movement patterns per competition event. Pick the events you want to train, then do all three patterns for that event — or cherry-pick across events. Aim for 4–6 patterns total per session. Warm up first; stop a set short if form breaks down.

Tap any movement's teal header to open it full-screen — easy to read on the gym iPad, pinch to zoom, scroll through all three exercises.

This week — second test wave. Peak block: comp-specific movements at competition standards on the remaining events. Volume stays low and sharp so you arrive fresh to each test. Everything is fresh this week; warm up fully, give one honest max per tested event, and keep the supporting work at 3 sets or fewer.
testsLineHtml: Test week — wave 2. Max-effort competition tests this week: Med Ball Slams, Jump Rope (60s), Concept Row 500m, and 300 Yd Shuttle. Warm up, take your best shot, and tap your name below to log each result. This completes the test waves — next week is comp rehearsal.

patterns.name: Comp-Pace Box Squat
patterns.rx: 3 × 8 reps · rest 2 min between sets · crisp and submaximal, well short of failure
patterns.load: Your competition KB at competition box height
cues: Hit comp weight at comp box height but stop each set feeling strong.
Make every rep clean — full sit, full stand, sharp turnaround.
Leave plenty in the tank; this is staying sharp, not testing.
Rest the full 2 minutes so each short set feels easy and fast.

patterns.name: KB Goblet Box Squat Singles
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 reps · rest 2 min between sets · one crisp rep at a time, reset between
patterns.load: Your competition KB, held at the chest, at competition box height
cues: Treat each of the 3 reps as its own clean effort with a quick reset.
Sit to the box, stand fast and tall, then settle before the next.
Keep it crisp and easy — stop well before any grind.
Focus on a perfect comp-pattern rep every single time.

patterns.name: Tall-Box Quick Squat
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 90 sec between sets · higher box, fast and easy standups
patterns.load: A light KB at the chest — keep it snappy and unloaded-feeling
cues: Use a box higher than comp height so the range is short and easy.
Stand up fast off the taller box to stay quick on your feet.
Keep it light and low-effort — this is sharpening, not loading.
Finish each set fresh, never fatigued.

patterns.name: Throw Technique Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 throws · rest 90 sec between sets · 9 crisp submaximal throws at comp standard, no maxing
patterns.load: Dynamax ball — 4 lb (women) / 8 lb (men)
cues: Hinge and explode up, throwing the ball up and back overhead — sharp and crisp, but not all-out this week.
Stay around 80–90% effort: rehearse perfect comp-day technique without digging into fatigue.
Drive from a fast hip snap and keep your arms loose to whip the ball over.
Reset fully between throws and keep every rep clean and controlled.

patterns.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridge
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps each leg · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight, lying on your back with one foot planted
cues: Lie on your back, plant one foot, and drive your hips up by squeezing the glute of the working leg, then lower under control.
Keep it light and supportive — this wakes up the glutes that power your throw without taxing you.
Keep your stomach braced so your lower back doesn't arch at the top.
Move smoothly and stop well short of fatigue.

patterns.name: Scarecrow External Rotation
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 reps · rest 45 sec between sets · light shoulder care
patterns.load: Very light DBs (2–5 lb) or no weight
cues: Raise your elbows to shoulder height, then rotate your hands up and back so your forearms point to the ceiling.
Keep it slow and very light — this keeps your throwing shoulders healthy heading into comp.
Squeeze your shoulder blades back gently and don't shrug up toward your ears.
Lower under control; quality and comfort over any load here.

patterns.name: Bench Volume @ % of Max Reps
patterns.rx: 2 sets · each set to ~40% of your max-rep test · rest 2 min between · easy and crisp
patterns.load: Your competition bench weight
cues: Your app shows your personal rep target — keep it light and crisp, well short of any grind.
This is sharp, low-volume touch work on the comp lift, not a hard session — leave plenty in the tank.
Stop the set the moment the bar slows or it stops feeling easy.

patterns.name: Tempo Incline Push-Up
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 75 sec between sets · slow and controlled
patterns.load: Bodyweight, hands on a bench or counter
cues: Place your hands on a bench so the angle is easy, then lower for a slow 3-count and press back up smoothly.
Keep your body in one straight line and the effort moderate — leave several reps in the tank.
Raise the hands higher to make it easier. Stop well before failure.

patterns.name: Seated DB Front Raise
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · light
patterns.load: Two light DBs you can raise easily for 10 reps
cues: Sit tall and raise two light DBs straight out in front to about shoulder height, then lower under control.
This is easy supportive shoulder work — keep it light and smooth with no swinging.
Stop the set well short of fatigue. Keep your torso still and let the shoulders do the work.

patterns.name: Max Hang Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × hold for time · rest 2 min between · keep each at ~70% of your best, low volume
patterns.load: Pull-up bar at a comfortable 70% of your best hang time — sharp and easy, no maxing this week
cues: Hold sub-max and crisp; this is sharpening, not a second test.
Pack the shoulders and crush the bar with spread fingers.
Step down well before true failure to stay fresh.
Log the holds but do not chase a number this week.

patterns.name: Light Inverted Hold
patterns.rx: 2 × hold for time · rest 90 sec between
patterns.load: Bar at hip height, body straight, heels on the floor — easy isometric hold of 15–20 seconds
cues: Set up under the bar and just hold the top position with shoulder blades pinched.
Keep the body straight from shoulders to heels — no sagging hips.
Hold easy and breathe; step down when the position softens.
Keep it light and supportive so the comp events stay the priority.

patterns.name: Light Wrist Curl
patterns.rx: 2 × 15 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light dumbbell or pair of dumbbells — easy forearm work, no straining
cues: Rest your forearms on your thighs and curl the wrists up and down slowly.
Keep the load light to flush the forearms, not fatigue them.
Full smooth range each rep — no jerking the weight.
Stop if the grip feels at all taxed; this week stays easy.

patterns.name: Standing Slam Volume
patterns.rx: 1 × 60 sec · max slams · full rest before and after
patterns.load: Your competition D-ball
cues: This is the test: as many clean slams as you can in 60 seconds at competition standard.
Go all-out, but every rep must reach full overhead and slam straight down to count.
Warm up thoroughly first, then empty the tank and log your final number.

patterns.name: Easy D-Ball Slam Primer
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: A light D-ball, 6 lb (women) / 12 lb (men)
cues: Before your test, slam a light ball easy and smooth to groove the pattern.
Keep it relaxed — this is a warm-up primer, not a workout.
Stop well short of any fatigue so the test gets your best effort.

patterns.name: Dead Bug
patterns.rx: 2 × 8 reps each side · rest 45 sec between sets
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: On your back, lower the opposite arm and leg slowly, then return without letting your low back arch.
Keep your ribs down and core gently braced the whole time.
Light supportive work only — keep everything else easy on test day.

patterns.name: 60-Sec Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 1 × 60 sec MAX singles for score · 1 all-out test set · full rest before and after
patterns.load: Your jump rope, all-out at competition standard — every clean single counts
cues: This is the test: count every clean single you complete in the full 60 seconds.
Open at a fast but holdable pace so you don't blow up in the first 20 seconds.
If you trip, get the rope moving again immediately — the clock keeps running.
Empty the tank in the final 15 seconds and finish on a sprint.

patterns.name: Primer Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps · rest 60 sec between sets · light and brisk
patterns.load: Light DB or KB in each hand, just enough to feel the calves switch on
cues: Do a few brisk, light raises to wake up the calves before your test.
Keep it easy — this primes, it does not fatigue.
Rise tall and lower with control, no grinding.

patterns.name: Tempo Warm-Up Skips
patterns.rx: 2 × 30 sec easy singles · rest 60 sec between sets · finish well before the test
patterns.load: Your jump rope, easy warm-up pace
cues: Use these easy sets to find your rhythm before the max test.
Stay relaxed and quiet on your feet to save your legs.
Rest fully after warming up so you hit the test fresh.

patterns.name: Submax Standing Jump-and-Stick
patterns.rx: 3 × 4 jumps · rest 90 sec between sets · about 80% effort, perfect landings
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Jump forward at a comfortable, submaximal effort — about 80 percent — and stick both feet softly each time.
Keep your volume low and your landings flawless; this week is light and supportive, not a max test.
Reset fully at the line between reps and stop the moment a landing feels less than clean.

patterns.name: Light Goblet March
patterns.rx: 3 × 30 sec · rest 60 sec between sets · tall and easy
patterns.load: A light KB or DB at your chest, easy enough to march for 30 seconds with good posture
cues: March in place lifting each knee to hip height, holding a light bell at your chest and staying tall.
Keep your stomach braced and your steps controlled — this is easy support work to stay loose.
Stay relaxed and stop early if your posture sags. Nothing about this should feel taxing.

patterns.name: Broad Jump — Distance Practice
patterns.rx: 3 × 3 jumps · rest 90 sec between sets · easy comp-pattern rehearsal
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Rehearse the competition motion at an easy effort — arms back, hips snap, jump and stick both feet.
Keep it low-volume and crisp; the goal this week is to stay sharp without digging into fatigue.
Reset at the line between each jump. Stop if the landings stop feeling clean and controlled.

patterns.name: 500m Practice
patterns.rx: 1 × 500m for time · ALL OUT · full warm-up first, then one max effort
patterns.load: Rower · maximum effort — this is the competition test, row 500m as fast as you can
cues: This is the real test — warm up fully, then row 500m as fast as you can for one piece.
Start strong but controlled; don't redline in the first 100m and blow up at the end.
Empty the tank on the last 150m — finish with everything you've got and record your time.

patterns.name: Easy Recovery Spin
patterns.rx: 1 × 5 min easy · very light, conversational
patterns.load: Rower · easy flush pace, well below race effort
cues: Spin easy to flush the legs before and after the test — keep it conversational.
Long, relaxed strokes; no pushing, this is recovery only.
Stop if you feel any strain — the goal is fresh legs, not more work.

patterns.name: Light Scarecrow Raise
patterns.rx: 2 × 10 reps · rest 60 sec between sets
patterns.load: Very light DBs or no weight — supportive shoulder prep only this week
cues: Hinge slightly, raise the arms out and rotate the hands up like a scarecrow, then lower slowly.
Keep it light and easy — this keeps the shoulders healthy, it's not a hard set.
Stop the set the moment your form gets sloppy; freshness is the goal this week.

patterns.name: Shuttle Pace Practice
patterns.rx: 1 × full 300 yd (6 × 50 yd) for time · one max effort · full warm-up first
patterns.load: Bodyweight · all-out competition effort — the real 300-yard shuttle for time
cues: Warm up thoroughly, then run the full 300 yards — six 50-yard lengths — as fast as you can for time.
Attack each turn with a controlled brake and explosive exit; pace the first lengths so you don't fade at the end.
Record your time. This is the comp test — one true max effort, nothing else taxing today.

patterns.name: Easy Shakeout Jog
patterns.rx: 2 × 60 yards · very easy jog · rest as needed
patterns.load: Bodyweight
cues: Jog two easy lengths to loosen up before and stay limber around your test effort.
Keep it conversational and relaxed — no speed, just blood flow.
This stays easy so all your energy goes into the timed test.

patterns.name: Standing Calf Raise
patterns.rx: 2 × 15 reps · slow controlled · rest 60 sec
patterns.load: Bodyweight, or hold a light 10–20 lb dumbbell in one hand
cues: Rise onto the balls of your feet, pause at the top, then lower slowly under control.
Keep a steady rhythm — this primes the ankles and calves without fatigue.
Light and supportive; the timed shuttle is the only hard work today.

events.tag: Peak + Taper · comp rehearsal (sled, not tested)

patterns.name: Comp-Distance Sled Push
patterns.rx: 3 × 50 yards · rest 3 min between · comp-pace rehearsal (not timed, never a test)
patterns.load: Competition load: 2 × 25 lb plates (women) / 2 × 45 lb plates (men)
cues: Push the full 50 yards at comp pace and comp weight, crisp and controlled — sled stays a training rehearsal, never a timed test.
Run your race setup: 45° body angle, arms locked, push through the trunk, long steps, drive through the toes.
Take full rest between pushes and keep effort moderate — the tested events get your fresh max this week.

patterns.name: Cossack Squat
patterns.rx: 2 × 6 reps per side · rest 75 sec between · light and controlled
patterns.load: Bodyweight or a very light weight held at your chest for balance
cues: Shift slowly side to side, sitting into one hip at a time to open your hips and keep your legs loose for the push.
Keep the heel of your bent leg down and your chest up — don't rush or bounce out of the bottom.
Go only as deep as feels comfortable; this is easy mobility-strength, not a hard set.

patterns.name: Banded Lateral Walk
patterns.rx: 2 × 12 steps per direction · rest 60 sec between · light activation
patterns.load: A light resistance band around your legs, just above the knees or ankles
cues: Sink into a quarter-squat and step sideways against the band to switch on the hips that stabilize your drive.
Keep tension on the band the whole time and your feet pointing forward — don't let your knees cave in.
Keep the steps small, slow, and easy; this is supportive prep, not fatigue work.
